response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable polyglot engineer with a strong background in TypeScript/Node.js, developer tooling, and systems work, but he is fundamentally misaligned with this role. The position requires deep Java/Kotlin expertise, Spring ecosystem fluency, and hands-on IDE internals experience — none of which are substantively demonstrated. Java appears briefly in his skills list and in peripheral tooling work at Altom, but there is no evidence of enterprise JVM development, Spring/Quarkus/Hibernate usage, Kotlin experience, or work on JVM-based IDE platforms. His most relevant adjacent experience (working on NI's C IDE and building test frameworks) is too distant from the IntelliJ/JVM tooling domain to compensate for the core qualification gaps.
strengths: Genuine developer-tooling mindset demonstrated through open-source test frameworks, MCP code generators, and IDE-adjacent work at National Instruments
Strong software craftsmanship ethos (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, clean architecture) that aligns with the quality standards expected in a large-scale IDE codebase
Broad polyglot experience across systems, backends, and tooling layers shows adaptability and low-level technical curiosity
concerns: No demonstrable Java or Kotlin depth — the role's primary language requirements; Java appears as a peripheral skill, never as a core production stack
Zero evidence of Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any enterprise JVM framework exposure across 15+ years of listed employment
No JVM IDE platform experience (IntelliJ Platform, Eclipse plugins, or equivalent); the NI C IDE stint was C/C++ based and does not transfer directly

key_factors.factor: No Spring or enterprise framework experience
